note: Six outage postmortems: three real public incidents condensed to markdown (Cloudflare 2022 BGP, Cloudflare 2019 WAF regex, GitLab 2017 database deletion) plus three synthetic 2025 internal-style incidents that introduce capacity-drain, online-migration lock, and config-drift failure modes for cross-incident synthesis.

entries.description: Cloudflare public postmortem: Cloudflare Outage — 2022-06-21 (BGP misconfiguration). Source: https://blog.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-outage-on-june-21-2022/
Cloudflare public postmortem: Cloudflare Outage — 2019-07-02 (WAF regex backtracking). Source: https://blog.cloudflare.com/details-of-the-cloudflare-outage-on-july-2-2019/
GitLab public postmortem: GitLab Outage — 2017-01-31 (Accidental primary database deletion). Source: https://about.gitlab.com/blog/postmortem-of-database-outage-of-january-31/
Synthetic but realistic ASG capacity-drain incident.
Synthetic but realistic online migration lock incident.
Synthetic but realistic hotfix config-drift incident.
